title: Annual Leave Policy
content: Annual leave entitlement is 21 days per year for employees with less than 5 years of service, and 30 days for employees with 5 or more years of service. Annual leave must be requested at least 7 days in advance through the Unified Workspace portal. Carry-over of unused leave is permitted up to a maximum of 10 days per year. Leave encashment is not permitted except upon resignation or termination. Emergency leave may be granted with retroactive approval in exceptional circumstances. Leave requests during peak periods (fiscal year-end, Hajj season) are subject to manager discretion and business needs.

title: Code of Conduct
content: All employees are expected to maintain the highest standards of professional conduct. This includes: treating all colleagues, clients, and partners with respect and dignity; maintaining confidentiality of company and client information; avoiding conflicts of interest and disclosing any potential conflicts to management; refraining from any form of discrimination, harassment, or bullying; using company resources responsibly and only for legitimate business purposes; reporting any violations of this code to HR or through the anonymous whistleblower channel. Violations may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination.

title: IT Security Policy 2026
content: All employees must comply with the following IT security requirements: Use strong passwords of at least 12 characters with a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols; passwords must be changed every 90 days. Do not share login credentials with anyone, including IT staff. Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on all company systems. Lock your computer screen when leaving your desk. Do not install unauthorized software on company devices. Be vigilant about phishing emails — verify sender identity before clicking links or downloading attachments. Report suspected security incidents to IT immediately. Company data must not be stored on personal devices or unapproved cloud services.

title: Remote Work Policy
content: Eligible employees may work remotely up to 2 days per week. Eligibility requirements: minimum 6 months of continuous service; satisfactory performance rating in the most recent appraisal; manager approval; role must be suitable for remote work as determined by the department head. Remote work days must be agreed with the manager in advance. Employees working remotely must be reachable during core hours (9 AM – 3 PM) and attend all mandatory meetings. Company data security policies apply regardless of work location. Remote work is a privilege, not a right, and can be revoked if performance is impacted.

title: Performance Appraisal Policy
content: Performance appraisals are conducted quarterly. The appraisal process consists of: (1) Self-Assessment: employee completes self-evaluation within the first 2 weeks of the new quarter; (2) Manager Assessment: line manager reviews self-assessment and provides their evaluation within the following 2 weeks; (3) One-on-One Discussion: manager and employee meet to discuss the appraisal, agree on scores, and set goals for the next quarter; (4) HR Review: HR reviews all completed appraisals for consistency. Ratings are on a scale of 1–5. Employees scoring below 2.5 will be placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP). Those scoring 4.5 and above are considered for promotion or salary review.

title: Sick Leave Policy
content: Employees are entitled to sick leave as follows: first 30 days of sick leave per year are paid at full salary; days 31–90 are paid at 75% salary; days 91–120 are unpaid. Sick leave must be reported to the manager on the first day of absence. For absences of 3 or more consecutive days, a medical certificate from a licensed physician is required. Sick leave during annual leave is not counted as annual leave and requires a medical certificate. Chronic illness cases are reviewed individually by HR. Abuse of sick leave policy may result in disciplinary action.
